DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Lifetime Achievement Award]] honoree on December 13, 2017.{{cite web |url=https://www.goldenglobes.com/articles/oprah-winfrey-recipient-cecil-b-demille-award-2018 |title=Oprah Winfrey: Recipient Of The Cecil B. deMille Award 2018 |website=www.goldenglobes.com |date=December 13, 2017 |access-date=December 14, 2017}}{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-42350747 |title=Oprah to get top Golden Globe honour |date=December 14, 2017 |work=BBC News |access-date=December 14, 2017}} The nominees were announced on December 11, 2017, by [[Sharon Stone]], [[Alfre Woodard]], [[Kristen Bell]] and [[Garrett Hedlund]]. During a pre-show event the award for "Best Podcast" was announced. The event was streamed live on YouTube. In support of the [[Me Too movement|#MeToo]] and [[Time's Up (organization)|Time's Up]] movements, practically all the attendees wore black.{{cite news |url=https://www.vox.com/identities/2018/1/8/16862512/golden-globes-oprah-winfrey-2018-metoo-harassment |title=The Golden Globes proved awards shows can be relevant in the era of #MeToo |work=Vox |access-date=2018-01-08}} Many of the acceptance speeches specifically mentioned these causes, including that of [[Oprah Winfrey]].{{cite news |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/pop-culture/awards/golden-globes-2018-metoo-casts-shadow-over-celebration-movies-tv-n835451 |title=#MeToo, powerful speeches dominate the 75th Golden Globe Awards |work=NBC News |access-date=2018-01-08}} === Presenters === {{div col|colwidth=30em}} * [[Gal Gadot]] and [[Dwayne Johnson]] with Best Actress – Miniseries or Television Film * [[Viola Davis]] and [[Helen Mirren]] with Best Supporting Actor – Motion Picture * [[Zac Efron]]{{cite news |last=Guglielmi |first=Jodi |title=Golden Globes: Kate Hudson, Zac Efron and Robert Pattinson Added to List of Presenters |url=http://people.com/movies/kate-hudson-zac-efron-golden-globes-presenters/ |date=January 5, 2018 |access-date=January 5, 2018 |journal=People}} introduced ''The Greatest Showman'' * [[Jennifer Aniston]]{{cite news |last=Johnson |first=Zach |title=Jennifer Aniston and Angelina Jolie at Golden Globes 2018 as presenters |url=http://www.eonline.com/news/903621/jennifer-aniston-and-angelina-jolie-to-present-at-the-2018-golden-globes |date=January 4, 2018 |access-date=January 4, 2018 |journal=E! |first=Yohana |last=Desta |title=Time's Up: How a Hollywood Initiative Is Tackling Sexual Predators |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2018/01/times-up-initiative-sexual-harassment-hollywood |magazine=Vanity Fair |date=January 2, 2018 |access-date=January 8, 2018}} [[Tarana Burke]], who created the [[Me Too (hashtag)|"Me too"]] movement in 2006, attended the awards as a guest of [[Michelle Williams (actress)|Michelle Williams]].{{cite web |author=CNWN Collection |url=https://www.allure.com/story/support-causes-activists-2018-golden-globes |title=Golden Globes 2018: How to King]] as a guest of [[Emma Stone]], [[Marai Larasi]] as a guest of [[Emma Watson]], [[Calina Lawrence]] as a guest of [[Shailene Woodley]], [[Ai-jen Poo]] as a guest of [[Meryl Streep]], and [[Mónica
